title: States move first as the AI buildout meets its limit - TCR 06/07/26
description:
New York banned hyperscale data centers over 20 megawatts for a year and Illinois froze new subsidy deals as the AI buildout becomes a top midterm issue, a self-replicating Miasma worm pushed credential-harvesting code into 73 Microsoft repositories that fires inside AI coding agents, and Lilly's retatrutide cut knee osteoarthritis pain 73% and sleep apnea severity 61% atop 28.3% weight loss at the ADA meeting.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-june-7-2026/

0:00 Miasma worm and OpenAI Lockdown Mode
1:55 New York and Illinois cap the AI buildout
6:54 A national security memo routes AI into the military
11:19 GLP-1 next wave at the ADA meeting
15:15 The Military Takes Control of the Model
19:08 Close

This episode covers the New York and Illinois data center policy shift, the anti-tech extremism rising at the buildout's violent fringe, the Miasma worm and OpenAI's Lockdown Mode response, the GLP-1 next wave with retatrutide, oral aleniglipron, and Pfizer's monthly berobenatide, a national security memo routing frontier AI into the military, and retired Waymo batteries becoming grid storage.

The spine of the edition is one shape: communities making the buildout's hidden costs visible from below while the federal layer consolidates frontier capability from above. New York wrote water and electricity reporting into law and Illinois froze subsidies, relocating costs that were externalized onto residents back onto the developers. The Miasma worm turned AI coding agents into both the target and the trigger of a credential heist, and the labs building that capability shipped containment in the same cycle. At the diabetes meeting, a triple-agonist obesity drug reached past weight into joint pain, sleep apnea, and blood sugar, while oral and monthly competitors widened the field. Retired robotaxi batteries return to the grids that once powered them, and an electrified MIT carbon-capture method points toward removal that runs on cheap renewable power.
